skill.name: chat-compactor
skill.description: Generate structured session summaries for AI agent handoffs. Use it to capture decisions, progress, failures, file changes, blockers, and next steps before a session ends.
skill.summary: Create structured handoff summaries so future AI sessions can resume work quickly.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: The static external command findings are false positives caused by markdown fences and inline code formatting. The weak cryptography finding at SKILL.md line 3 is also a false positive because the line is only descriptive metadata. The skill has low filesystem risk because it instructs agents to save session summaries that may contain sensitive project context.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported backticks appear in markdown fences, inline filenames, and an example response. They are not executable shell commands and do not create command injection risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations contain markdown formatting and literal example filenames. No code path, interpreter call, or user-controlled command execution is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is Descriptive Text
low_findings.description: The reported line is the YAML description for the skill. It does not contain cryptographic code, hashing logic, or references to a weak algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is plain metadata describing the skill purpose. I found no evidence of cryptographic operations in that line.

low_findings.title: Session Summary Files May Persist Sensitive Context
low_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to save markdown summaries in the project root or a sessions directory. This is legitimate behavior, but summaries may include private project details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file-writing instruction is explicit and part of the intended workflow. The risk depends on the sensitivity of the captured session content.

content.value_statement: Long AI sessions often lose decisions, blockers, and project state. This skill creates structured handoff summaries that help Claude, Codex, and Claude Code resume work with less context loss.
actual_capabilities: Summarizes completed work, decisions, blockers, and next steps.
Captures failed approaches so future agents avoid repeated work.
Produces a consistent markdown handoff structure.
Identifies modified files and environment notes from the session.
Creates suggested filenames for saved session summaries.
limitations: It depends on conversation history available to the agent.
It does not verify file changes outside the current context.
It may preserve sensitive details if the session contained them.
It does not manage storage, retention, or access controls.

use_cases.title: End a Coding Session
use_cases.description: Create a clear handoff before stopping work on a feature, bug fix, or refactor.

use_cases.title: Resume Debugging Later
use_cases.description: Record failed attempts, current hypotheses, blockers, and the next useful diagnostic step.

use_cases.title: Transfer Context Between Agents
use_cases.description: Prepare a compact project state summary for another AI assistant or team member.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Session Wrap-Up
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this when a session is ending.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compact this session into a handoff summary. Include what changed, current state, blockers, and next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Debugging Handoff
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this after investigating a difficult bug.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a debugging handoff. Emphasize hypotheses tested, dead ends, evidence found, and the next diagnostic action.

prompt_templates.title: Implementation Continuity
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this before switching tasks during active development.
prompt_templates.prompt: Summarize this implementation session for the next agent. Include key files, design choices, tests run, and unfinished work.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Review Handoff
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this for complex work with decisions and risks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a concise handoff with decisions, rejected alternatives, modified files, validation results, risks, and prioritized next actions.

output_examples.input: Wrap up this authentication refactor session for tomorrow.
output_examples.output: A handoff summary with the refactor status, changed authentication files, design decisions, tests run, open risks, and next tasks.

output_examples.input: Compact this debugging session so another agent can continue.
output_examples.output: A debugging summary that lists symptoms, confirmed facts, failed fixes, likely causes, blockers, and the next diagnostic command.

output_examples.input: Save context before we switch from backend work to frontend work.
output_examples.output: A transition summary that captures backend progress, pending integration points, relevant files, and the first frontend follow-up task.

best_practices: Review summaries for secrets before saving or sharing them.
Ask for file paths, tests, blockers, and next steps explicitly.
Use the summary at task boundaries, not only at session end.
anti_patterns: Do not include long narrative history when a compact state summary is enough.
Do not omit failed approaches, because they prevent repeated investigation.
Do not save summaries containing credentials or private customer data.

faq.question: What does this skill produce?
faq.answer: It produces a structured markdown handoff summary for future AI or human continuation.

faq.question: Does it execute commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill text only defines a summary workflow and markdown output format.

faq.question: Can it save files?
faq.answer: Yes. It instructs the agent to save a markdown session summary when appropriate.

faq.question: Is it useful outside coding tasks?
faq.answer: Yes. It can summarize research, planning, or operations sessions that need continuity.

faq.question: What should users check before saving?
faq.answer: Users should remove secrets, customer data, private notes, and unnecessary sensitive context.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
